message:
fix: bind yq path keys via strenv() across remaining helpers

Five spots still spliced bash variables directly into yq path
expressions, all from earlier agents' code:

  dsr           _build_get_archive_format  (\$tool, \$os)
  dsr           _build_get_include_files   (\$tool)
  host_selector selector_get_limit         (\$hostname)
  host_selector candidate enumeration      (\$hostname x2)
  quality_gates qg_get_checks              (\$tool_name)

When the spliced value contains a yq metachar (`.`, `[`, `(`, …),
the path expression silently matches a different key or evaluates
an unintended sub-expression — the same defense-in-depth class
that round 4 fixed in src/config.sh and that I noted as still
present in this file during round 5.

In current practice none of these inputs carry yq metachars (tool
names are validated upstream by act_load_repo_config; host names
come from hosts.yaml; os values are linux|darwin|windows), so this
is hardening rather than an active exploit fix.  Bound each value
through an env var + strenv(), matching the established pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2269,29 +2269,35 @@ EOF
         local config_dir="${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}"
         local tool_config="$config_dir/repos.d/${tool}.yaml"
 
+        # NOTE: $tool and $os are interpolated via env vars + strenv()
+        # rather than spliced directly into the yq path expression.
+        # Same defense-in-depth class as the round-4 config.sh fix:
+        # if either ever contains a yq metachar (`.`, `[`, `(`, …)
+        # the directly-spliced form silently returns the wrong value
+        # or evaluates an unintended expression.
         if command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
             if [[ -f "$tool_config" ]]; then
-                format=$(yq -r ".archive_format.${os} // \"\"" "$tool_config" 2>/dev/null)
+                format=$(DSR_OS="$os" yq -r '.archive_format[strenv(DSR_OS)] // ""' "$tool_config" 2>/dev/null)
                 [[ "$format" == "null" ]] && format=""
                 if [[ -n "$format" ]]; then
                     echo "$format"
                     return 0
                 fi
-                format=$(yq -r ".archive_format // \"\"" "$tool_config" 2>/dev/null)
+                format=$(yq -r '.archive_format // ""' "$tool_config" 2>/dev/null)
                 if [[ "$format" != "null" && "$format" != "" && "$format" != "{"* ]]; then
                     echo "$format"
                     return 0
                 fi
             fi
 
             if [[ -f "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" ]]; then
-                format=$(yq -r ".tools.${tool}.archive_format.${os} // \"\"" "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
+                format=$(DSR_TOOL="$tool" DSR_OS="$os" yq -r '.tools[strenv(DSR_TOOL)].archive_format[strenv(DSR_OS)] // ""' "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
                 [[ "$format" == "null" ]] && format=""
                 if [[ -n "$format" ]]; then
                     echo "$format"
                     return 0
                 fi
-                format=$(yq -r ".tools.${tool}.archive_format // \"\"" "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
+                format=$(DSR_TOOL="$tool" yq -r '.tools[strenv(DSR_TOOL)].archive_format // ""' "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)
                 if [[ "$format" != "null" && "$format" != "" && "$format" != "{"* ]]; then
                     echo "$format"
                     return 0
@@ -2316,7 +2322,10 @@ EOF
                 return 0
             fi
             if [[ -f "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" ]]; then
-                yq -r ".tools.${tool}.include_files[]? // empty" "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null
+                # See _build_get_archive_format above — same strenv()
+                # rationale: $tool came from a CLI flag and may be
+                # unsanitised at the boundary.
+                DSR_TOOL="$tool" yq -r '.tools[strenv(DSR_TOOL)].include_files[]? // empty' "$DSR_REPOS_FILE" 2>/dev/null
                 return 0
             fi
         fi

@@ -61,8 +61,13 @@ selector_get_limit() {
         return 0
     fi
 
+    # Use strenv() to bind $hostname into the yq path safely; spliced
+    # interpolation would let a hostname containing a yq metachar
+    # (`.`, `[`, `(`, …) silently match the wrong key or evaluate an
+    # unintended expression.  Same defense-in-depth class as the
+    # round-4 config.sh fix.
     local limit
-    limit=$(yq -r ".hosts.${hostname}.concurrency // $_SELECTOR_DEFAULT_MAX_PARALLEL" "$hosts_file" 2>/dev/null)
+    limit=$(DSR_HOST="$hostname" yq -r ".hosts[strenv(DSR_HOST)].concurrency // $_SELECTOR_DEFAULT_MAX_PARALLEL" "$hosts_file" 2>/dev/null)
     echo "${limit:-$_SELECTOR_DEFAULT_MAX_PARALLEL}"
 }
 
@@ -291,11 +296,13 @@ selector_get_candidates() {
     while IFS= read -r hostname; do
         [[ -z "$hostname" ]] && continue
 
-        # Get host info
+        # Get host info.  See selector_get_limit above — we bind
+        # $hostname through strenv() so a yq-metachar in the
+        # hostname (`.`, `[`, …) can't reshape the path expression.
         local platform="" connection=""
         if [[ -f "$hosts_file" ]] && command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
-            platform=$(yq -r ".hosts.${hostname}.platform // \"\"" "$hosts_file" 2>/dev/null)
-            connection=$(yq -r ".hosts.${hostname}.connection // \"ssh\"" "$hosts_file" 2>/dev/null)
+            platform=$(DSR_HOST="$hostname" yq -r '.hosts[strenv(DSR_HOST)].platform // ""' "$hosts_file" 2>/dev/null)
+            connection=$(DSR_HOST="$hostname" yq -r '.hosts[strenv(DSR_HOST)].connection // "ssh"' "$hosts_file" 2>/dev/null)
         fi
 
         # Filter by target platform if specified

@@ -54,8 +54,14 @@ qg_get_checks() {
         return 0
     fi
 
+    # Use strenv() to bind $tool_name into the yq path safely.
+    # $tool_name comes from `dsr quality --tool <name>` (CLI arg) so
+    # could in theory carry yq metachars (`.`, `[`, `(`, …); spliced
+    # interpolation would silently match the wrong key or evaluate
+    # an unintended expression.  Same defense-in-depth class as the
+    # round-4 config.sh fix.
     local checks
-    checks=$(yq -o=json ".tools.${tool_name}.checks // []" "$repos_file" 2>/dev/null) || checks='[]'
+    checks=$(DSR_TOOL="$tool_name" yq -o=json '.tools[strenv(DSR_TOOL)].checks // []' "$repos_file" 2>/dev/null) || checks='[]'
     echo "$checks"
 }
